swamiji.title: Swami Mukundananda
swamiji.subtitle: Early life, education, and the path to spiritual awakening
swamiji.earlyP1: Swami Mukundananda was born on 19 December, 1960, and spent his formative years in various parts of India. From a very young age, he displayed a deep inclination toward spirituality. Even as a child, he was naturally drawn to meditation and contemplation, reflecting an innate thirst for life's eternal truths.
swamiji.earlyP2: His brilliance led him to two of India's most prestigious institutions—earning him a degree in Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT Delhi) and later an MBA from the Indian Institute of Management (IIM Calcutta).
swamiji.earlyP3: During his academic years, he immersed himself in the writings of saints and spiritual scriptures, which intensified his longing for higher realisation. By the time he graduated, his hunger for spiritual awakening had grown ever stronger, and he began to follow the path of Shree Krishna Bhakti.
swamiji.journeyP1: After taking up the order of Sanyas, he embarked on a spiritual journey that led him to the holy land of Braj and many other sacred places across India. His experiences with enlightened and scholarly saints only strengthened his resolve. Determined to find a true spiritual master, he continued his journey for many years, learning from the holy saints he met along the way.
swamiji.journeyP2: During these travels, he closely associated with many elevated saints of India, studied the writings of the great Acharyas of the past, and practiced intense devotion.
